code_usability.detail: No executable code generated; Mode A writing skill

reliability.note: Core structure solid; font/size recommendations are general best practice, not conference-verified

agent_usability.note: Feedback design: no confirmation step between story extraction and section plan delivery

agent_specific.note: Description precise; composability limited (poster planning is typically a standalone task)

key_strengths: Space allocation guidelines (Introduction 10-15% / Results 35-45%) are practically calibrated and rare in academic tools
Figure selection strategy (Must-include/Should-include/Cut-for-poster) gives direct actionable guidance
Text compression table per section with word count targets converts vague advice into measurable goals
Design accessibility tip (colorblind-safe color scheme) is a valuable and often-overlooked addition

recommendations.title: Section hierarchy and font size recommendations not verified for specific conference poster size standards
recommendations.problem: Font size recommendations (title ≥72pt, headers ≥36pt) are general best practices but may not match specific conference-mandated poster sizes or templates.
recommendations.root_cause: Design guidance is general rather than conference-specific.
recommendations.fix: Add a conference-specific note: 'Font sizes and section proportions are guidelines for standard A0/36x48 inch posters. Always verify against the target conference's poster specifications.'

recommendations.title: No guidance on QR code content best practices
recommendations.problem: The skill recommends adding a QR code but does not specify what it should link to (preprint vs full paper vs project page vs contact info) or how to generate it.
recommendations.root_cause: QR code recommendation is added without actionable implementation detail.
recommendations.fix: Expand QR code guidance: 'Link the QR code to a preprint (bioRxiv/medRxiv), published paper DOI, or project landing page. Use a stable short URL (e.g., bit.ly or DOI redirect). Test the link before printing.'
